mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.0% and remains there for at least 4 weeks
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 1.55% suggests easing bias
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration that precedes equity market weakness. Negative payroll surprises trigger a 5-trading-day negative return signal as investors anticipate recession risk.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month by more than 50,000 persons
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 152,186K shows robust employment
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts below 1,500K units signals weakening housing demand that foreshadows broader consumer spending contraction. This demand shock produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,500K and remains below this level for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1,608K is above threshold but near critical level
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances, or cumulative Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power. This supply strength supports corporate profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 109.2026 reflects solid industrial activity
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of all periods when INDPRO growth exceeds 2.5% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with declining unemployment creates a demand-labor interaction: strong consumer spending (RSAFS) paired with tight labor markets (UNRATE < 4.0%) signals inflation pressure and Fed tightening risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as rate expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS grows month-over-month by > 1.0% AND UNRATE remains below 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 529,766M and UNRATE at 3.6% show demand-labor tightness
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window despite interaction specification.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI accelerates above 3.0% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.5%, creating a negative real rate regime. This monetary accommodation paradox signals policy lag and eventual tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors anticipate rate hikes.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 3.0% AND FEDFUNDS < 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 258.82 and FEDFUNDS at 1.55% show potential policy lag
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rising inflation
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.5% signaling labor market deterioration and recession risk. This threshold crossing triggers a negative signal for S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors immediately reprice equity risk premiums upward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 3.6% is well below recession threshold
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerates below 2.0% annualized while retail sales remain flat or declining, signaling demand-side weakness across both aggregate and consumer segments. This dual demand weakness produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0% annualized AND RSAFS shows month-over-month decline
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 19,219.767B and RSAFS at 529,766M show solid demand
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window despite interaction specification.
